key_findings: RL-based notification personalization shows positive but modest effects on PA across multiple populations (DIAMANTE, Doherty 2024, Song 2025)
Ghosh et al. (2024) resampling framework reveals HeartSteps V2 personalization may partially be artifact of algorithm stochasticity
LLM-enhanced state representations for RL in JITAIs show 15-25% policy learning improvements in simulation (Karine & Marlin 2025)
Hybrid cMABxLLM approaches (Song 2025) offer deployable template combining Bayesian adaptive experimentation with generative models
Offline RL methods (KANDI, Lin 2026) emerge as path to learn PA policies from existing large-scale datasets (All of Us, PEER)
Negative result (Kurisu 2026): single-arm RL app showed no significant PA improvement, highlighting feasibility gaps
Contextual bandit protocols for PA (Caro 2025, MoveMentor) proliferating but results pending; next 12-24 months critical for evidence
Field moving toward richer state spaces (LLM-inferred, sensor-based) and flexible action spaces (continuous step distributions, generative content)
Most published RL-for-PA trials remain small (n<100) or simulation-based; PEARL (n=13,463) remains the scale benchmark
Scoping reviews identify RL as dominant ML approach for adaptive PA but note significant methodological heterogeneity and lack of standardized evaluation

03.key_findings: Contextual bandits are increasingly deployed in healthcare beyond physical activity, with major trials in mental health (N=1,282), medication adherence (REINFORCE), and clinical trial optimization. Key trends: Thompson sampling dominates; fairness/equity constraints emerging; practical deployment challenges documented; negative results highlight need for careful evaluation.

papers.label: Effectiveness of a Digital Health Intervention Leveraging Reinforcement Learning: Results From the Diabetes and Mental Health Adaptive Notification Tracking and Evaluation (DIAMANTE) Randomized Clinical Trial
papers.authors: Aguilera A, Arevalo Avalos M, Xu J, Chakraborty B, Figueroa C, Garcia F, Rosales K, Hernandez-Ramos R, Karr C, Williams J, Ochoa-Frongia L, Sarkar U, Yom-Tov E, Lyles C
papers.headline: RL-based notification personalization improved PA in diabetes/depression patients
papers.detail: RCT using RL to personalize digital health notifications for Spanish-speaking adults with diabetes/depression. RL arm significantly improved physical activity and mental health outcomes compared to control.
papers.effect_size: Significant PA improvement (p<0.05); primary outcomes were behavioral and mental health
papers.notes: RL for multi-behavior intervention. Complements PEARL in clinical population. Published JMIR Oct 2024.

papers.label: Machine Learning Methods to Personalize Persuasive Strategies in mHealth Interventions That Promote Physical Activity: Scoping Review and Categorization Overview
papers.authors: Brons A, Wang S, Visser B, Krose B, Bakkes S, Veltkamp R
papers.headline: RL most common ML approach for timing personalization in PA mHealth, but effect sizes modest
papers.detail: Scoping review of ML methods for personalizing persuasive strategies in PA mHealth interventions. Found RL was the dominant approach for timing of messages. Provides taxonomy of persuasion strategies and ML personalization methods.
papers.notes: Provides taxonomy of ML-for-PA personalization. Valuable for situating our config-driven framework in the landscape.

papers.label: A scoping review of methodologies for applying artificial intelligence to physical activity interventions
papers.authors: An R, Shen J, Wang J, Yang Y
papers.venue: Journal of Sport and Health Science
papers.headline: RL, ML, and DL all applied to PA intervention design; RL most promising for adaptive personalization
papers.detail: Comprehensive scoping review of AI methodologies for PA interventions. Found RL applied mostly for adaptive intervention delivery and personalization. Notes significant heterogeneity in methodology and outcome reporting.
papers.notes: Published online 2023, in print 2024. High-impact sports science journal.

papers.label: An Evaluation of the Effect of App-Based Exercise Prescription Using Reinforcement Learning on Satisfaction and Exercise Intensity: Randomized Crossover Trial
papers.authors: Doherty C, Lambe R, O'Grady B, O'Reilly-Morgan D, Smyth B, Lawlor A, Hurley N, Tragos E
papers.headline: RL-based exercise prescription improved user satisfaction and exercise intensity vs fixed schedules
papers.detail: Randomized crossover trial comparing RL-based exercise prescription against fixed schedules. RL personalized workout difficulty and type. Found significantly higher user satisfaction and self-reported exercise intensity in RL arm.
papers.effect_size: Higher satisfaction scores (Cohen's d ~0.5-0.7); higher Borg RPE in RL arm
papers.notes: One of few RL-for-exercise trials with positive results. Small sample but crossover design. Published Nov 2024.

papers.label: Did we personalize? Assessing personalization by an online reinforcement learning algorithm using resampling
papers.authors: Ghosh S, Kim R, Chhabria P, Dwivedi R, Klasnja P, Liao P, Zhang K, Murphy S
papers.headline: Resampling-based test reveals HeartSteps RL personalization may be artifact of algorithm stochasticity
papers.detail: Introduces resampling methodology to assess whether an RL algorithm truly personalizes treatments. Applied to HeartSteps V2 data. Finds apparent personalization in certain states may be artifact of RL stochasticity rather than genuine learning.
papers.notes: Directly relevant to HeartSteps V2. Top ML journal. Authors include Murphy group leaders.

papers.label: Smartphone App Using Reinforcement Learning for Obesity: Single-Arm Feasibility Study
papers.authors: Kurisu K, Yamamoto Y, Aoyama T, Yamauchi T, Yoshiuchi K
papers.headline: Single-arm RL app for obesity showed no significant PA improvement; feasibility established
papers.detail: Single-arm feasibility study of smartphone app using RL for personalized behavioral recommendations targeting weight loss and PA. Small sample, no control group. PA outcomes not significantly improved from baseline. Habituation and small sample cited as limitations.
papers.effect_size: No significant change in PA levels; small weight loss (1.2 kg, p=0.08)
papers.notes: Explicitly reports null finding for PA outcomes. Important negative result for RL-for-PA literature. Feb 2026.

papers.label: Impact of Iterative Development and Beta-Testing on the Usability and Acceptability of a Novel Just-in-Time Adaptive Digital Physical Activity Intervention
papers.authors: Vandelanotte C, Maher C, Hodgetts D, Imam T, Rashid M, To QG, Trost S
papers.venue: Journal of Physical Activity and Health
papers.headline: MoveMentor JITAI with ML back-end shows acceptable usability but mixed engagement
papers.detail: Usability/acceptability evaluation of MoveMentor, a JITAI digital assistant using ML/RL for PA message personalization. Beta-testing showed acceptable usability (SUS ~68) but variable contextual engagement.
papers.effect_size: SUS score 68/100; engagement varied by context
papers.notes: Companion to MoveMentor protocol. Valuable for real-world deployment challenges. Published Jun 2025.

papers.label: MoveMentor - examining effectiveness of a machine learning and app-based digital assistant to increase physical activity in adults: protocol for a randomised controlled trial
papers.authors: Vandelanotte C, Trost S, Hodgetts D, Imam T, Rashid M, To QG, Maher C
papers.headline: Protocol for RCT of ML-based JITAI digital assistant for PA (MoveMentor)
papers.detail: Two-arm RCT (n=300) evaluating ML/RL-based PA message personalization vs static comparison. Primary outcome: daily step count change at 12 weeks.
papers.notes: Important upcoming trial. Results expected 2026-2027. Published Trials Jul 2025.

papers.label: Automated Personalized Goal Setting for Individual Exercise Behavior: Protocol for a Web-Based Adaptive Intervention Trial
papers.authors: Caro JC, Nguyen PH, Lipman S
papers.headline: Protocol for contextual bandit-based goal recommendation for exercise (Apptivate)
papers.detail: Protocol for adaptive intervention trial using contextual bandits for personalized goal setting. Three arms: user choice, CB recommendations, automated plans. n=500, 40-day intervention.
papers.notes: Directly uses contextual bandits for PA. Addresses user autonomy question. Results expected 2026.

papers.label: Promoting physical activity in middle-aged and older adults through digital intervention: matching message frame and recipient profile through deep reinforcement learning
papers.authors: Catellani P, Piastra M
papers.headline: DRL message framing matched to user profiles improves PA motivation in older adults
papers.detail: Proposes deep RL framework for matching message framing (gain vs loss) to user psychological profiles in PA intervention for older adults. Simulation-based evaluation shows 15-20% engagement improvement over static assignment.
papers.effect_size: Simulated engagement improvement ~15-20% over static assignment
papers.notes: Uses DRL not contextual bandits. Simulation-based. Targets older adults. Published 2025.

papers.label: A Reinforcement Learning Approach to Enhance Physical Activity and Reduce Fall Risk in Older Adults
papers.authors: Liu C, Thiamwong L, Nguyen T, Suarez JR, Park JH, Lou Q, Xie R
papers.headline: RL-based wearable feedback system for PA and fall risk in older adults (preliminary mixed results)
papers.detail: Conference abstract presenting RL using wearable sensors for real-time PA feedback in older adults. RL agent learned personalized activity suggestions. 60% showed positive step trends, 40% showed no change.
papers.effect_size: 60% of participants showed positive step count trends, 40% showed no change
papers.notes: Conference abstract. Full paper may be separate. Related to KANDI paper by same group.

papers.label: Tailored Behavior-Change Messaging for Physical Activity: Integrating Contextual Bandits and Large Language Models
papers.authors: Song H, Hofer D, Islambouli R, Hawkins L, Bhattacharjee A, Hassanzadeh Z, Smeddinck J, Franklin M, Williams JJ
papers.headline: Hybrid cMABxLLM approach balances personalization and token efficiency in 30-day PA trial
papers.detail: Deployed hybrid cMAB+LLM system in 30-day PA intervention. Compared 5 delivery models: randomization, cMAB-only, LLM-only, LLM+history, cMABxLLM. cMABxLLM matched LLM acceptance with ~60% fewer tokens, improved delivery balance across 4 intervention types.
papers.effect_size: cMABxLLM matched LLM-only message acceptance with ~60% fewer tokens
papers.notes: First deployed cMABxLLM hybrid for PA. Highly relevant to LLM+RL next steps. v4 Mar 2026.

papers.label: Enhancing Adaptive Behavioral Interventions with LLM Inference from Participant-Described States
papers.authors: Karine K, Marlin BM
papers.headline: LLM-inferred state descriptions improve RL policy learning in PA intervention simulation by 15-25%
papers.detail: Proposes using LLMs to infer richer state representations from participant natural language, expanding state space without reducing data efficiency. Shows LLM-inferred states significantly improve online policy learning for RL-based JITAI. Extension of StepCountJITAI framework.
papers.effect_size: Policy learning improved 15-25% with LLM-inferred states in simulation
papers.notes: Accepted at MLHC 2025. Direct extension of StepCountJITAI. Highly relevant to sensor FM + LLM next steps.

papers.label: Diffusion Policies with Offline and Inverse Reinforcement Learning for Promoting Physical Activity in Older Adults Using Wearable Sensors
papers.authors: Liu C, Thiamwong L, Fu Y, Xie R
papers.headline: KANDI: Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks + Diffusion Policies for offline IRL in PA promotion outperform SOTA
papers.detail: Introduces KANDI for offline inverse RL applied to PA in older adults at fall risk. Learns reward functions from expert behavior. Uses diffusion policies in actor-critic. Evaluated on PEER study data. Outperforms SOTA on D4RL benchmark.
papers.effect_size: Outperforms baseline offline RL methods on D4RL; improved PA alignment with expert behavior
papers.notes: Accepted ICMLA 2025. Offline RL + IRL for PA. Novel use of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks. Preprint.

papers.label: Precision Physical Activity Prescription via Reinforcement Learning for Functional Actions
papers.authors: Lin G, Miao R, Sacheck J, Zhang X
papers.headline: Offline RL learns personalized optimal step distributions from All of Us data (n~5000)
papers.detail: Offline RL algorithm for learning personalized optimal daily step distributions over time, using All of Us data. Continuous action space (step distribution function). Learned policy recommends ~20% more steps with more consistent patterns. Subgroup-specific recommendations by glucose, BMI, BP, age, sex.
papers.effect_size: Learned policy recommends ~20% more steps on average vs observed behavior
papers.notes: First RL-PA paper with continuous functional action space. Real All of Us data. Preprint May 2026.

papers.label: A novel physical activity recognition approach using deep ensemble optimized transformers and reinforcement learning
papers.authors: Ahmadian S, Rostami M, Farrahi V, Oussalah M
papers.headline: RL-optimized transformer ensemble for PA recognition (94% accuracy)
papers.detail: Uses RL to optimize ensemble of transformers for PA recognition from wearable data. Activity recognition focus, not intervention design. Demonstrates RL use in PA domain for model optimization.
papers.notes: Activity recognition, not intervention. Tangential. Published Neural Networks 2024.

papers.label: Personalized Type 1 Diabetes Management: Reinforcement Learning-Based Insulin Dosing and Glucose Forecasting
papers.authors: Taku EM, Gupta V, Singhal A
papers.headline: RL for insulin dosing uses PA as context variable; reduces hypoglycemic events 30% in simulation
papers.detail: RL-based insulin dosing system using PA as context variable affecting glucose. PA promotion not the goal, but demonstrates PA-aware RL for health. Reduced hypoglycemic events ~30% vs static regimen in simulation.
papers.effect_size: 30% reduction in hypoglycemic events in simulation
papers.notes: PA is context feature not intervention target. Tangential. Published JMIR Diabetes Jun 2026.

papers.label: Artificial Intelligence-Enabled Mobile Health Intervention (mDiabetes) to Reduce Diabetes Risk Behaviors in Rural India: Quasi-Experimental Pre-Post Study
papers.authors: Chadwick J, Jaswal N, Surya J, Sivamani C, Ramesan V, Saligram N
papers.headline: AI-powered personalized messages improved PA and diet in rural India (n=1048 pre-post)
papers.detail: Quasi-experimental pre-post study of AI-enabled mHealth for diabetes risk reduction in rural India. AI personalized message content. Improved PA and dietary outcomes vs static messages. Uses rule-based AI, not RL specifically.
papers.effect_size: Significant improvement in self-reported PA and dietary behaviors
papers.notes: AI-personalized not RL-specific. Large sample. Pre-post design limits inference. Dec 2025.

papers.label: A Scoping Review on Artificial Intelligence-Supported Interventions for Nonpharmacologic Management of Chronic Rheumatic Diseases
papers.authors: Shah N, Castellanos A, Chen Y, Piette J, Bucher A, Murphy S
papers.headline: AI-supported PA interventions for rheumatic diseases show promise but limited RL evidence
papers.detail: Scoping review of AI-supported nonpharmacologic interventions (including PA) for chronic rheumatic diseases. Limited RL use; most AI used simpler ML methods. Identifies opportunity for RL in PA for arthritis patients.
papers.notes: Reviews AI for PA in rheumatic disease. Limited direct RL relevance. Published Feb 2026.

papers.label: The Micro-Randomized Trial for Developing Digital Interventions: Data Analysis Methods
papers.authors: Tianchen Qian, Michael A. Russell, Linda M. Collins, Predrag Klasnja, Stephanie T. Lanza, Hyesun Yoo, Susan A. Murphy
papers.headline: Introduced the WCLS estimator for causal excursion effects in MRTs with worked HeartSteps examples
papers.detail: Provides formal definition of causal excursion effects for MRT data analysis and introduces the weighted and centered least-squares (WCLS) estimator. Covers primary and secondary analyses with SAS/R implementation. Uses HeartSteps MRT as running example throughout.
papers.notes: Foundational MRT methods paper; defines causal excursion effects and WCLS estimator used across all subsequent MRT analyses.

papers.label: Multi-Level Micro-Randomized Trial: Detecting the Proximal Effect of Messages on Physical Activity
papers.authors: Jing Xu, Xiaoxi Yan, Caroline Figueroa, Joseph Jay Williams, Bibhas Chakraborty
papers.headline: Extended MRT design to multi-level intervention components with GEE-based sample size calculators
papers.detail: Proposes multi-level MRT (MLMRT) design allowing more than two levels per intervention component. Develops GEE-based test statistics for proximal effects and associated sample size calculators. Motivated by DIAMANTE smartphone study for physical activity in diabetes and mental health. R Shiny app provided.
papers.notes: First paper to extend MRT framework beyond binary treatments; R Shiny app for sample size calculation.

papers.label: Assessing Time-Varying Causal Effect Moderation in the Presence of Cluster-Level Treatment Effect Heterogeneity
papers.authors: Jieru Shi, Zhenke Wu, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Extended causal excursion effects to cluster-level heterogeneity and interference in MRTs
papers.detail: Revisits causal excursion effects under potential cluster-level treatment effect heterogeneity and interference. Tests moderation by cluster-level variables using data from multi-institution Intern Health Study MRT of first-year medical residents.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Intern Health Study analysis
papers.notes: First MRT paper to relax between-subject independence and non-interference assumptions.

papers.label: The Micro-Randomized Trial for Developing Digital Interventions: Experimental Design and Data Analysis Considerations
papers.authors: Tianchen Qian, Ashley E. Walton, Linda M. Collins, Predrag Klasnja, Stephanie T. Lanza, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Mashifiqui Rabbi, Michael A. Russell, Maureen A. Walton, Hyesun Yoo, Susan A. Murphy
papers.headline: Comprehensive tutorial on why, when, and how to use MRTs for JITAI development
papers.detail: Comprehensive review covering MRT planning, design, implementation, primary and secondary analyses. Covers causal excursion effects, WCLS estimator, and software implementation. Uses HeartSteps, SARA, and BariFit MRTs as illustrations. Clarifies design considerations for researchers new to MRTs.
papers.notes: Definitive tutorial for practitioners; overlaps with arXiv:2004.10241 and arXiv:2005.05880.

papers.label: Estimating Time-Varying Direct and Indirect Causal Excursion Effects with Longitudinal Binary Outcomes
papers.authors: Jieru Shi, Zhenke Wu, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Extended MRT causal framework to decompose direct and indirect effects through time-varying mediators
papers.detail: Develops methodology for decomposing total causal excursion effects into direct and indirect effects in MRTs with binary outcomes. Addresses time-varying mediation in mobile health intervention settings where intermediate variables may be affected by treatment.
papers.notes: Bridges causal mediation analysis with the MRT causal excursion effect framework.

papers.label: A Meta-Learning Method for Estimation of Causal Excursion Effects to Assess Time-Varying Moderation
papers.authors: Jieru Shi, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Proposed doubly robust DR-WCLS estimator handling unknown randomization, missing data, and high-dimensional features
papers.detail: Proposes DR-WCLS, a flexible doubly robust inferential procedure for causal excursion effects from a meta-learner perspective. Handles uncertain randomization probabilities, missing observations, and high-dimensional mHealth data. Demonstrates consistent and more efficient estimates than existing WCLS methods.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Intern Health Study analysis
papers.notes: Key advance: DR-WCLS relaxes three major WCLS assumptions simultaneously.

papers.label: Incorporating Auxiliary Variables to Improve the Efficiency of Time-Varying Treatment Effect Estimation
papers.authors: Jieru Shi, Zhenke Wu, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Showed auxiliary variable adjustment improves MRT estimation efficiency with local efficiency guarantees
papers.detail: Proposes method to incorporate auxiliary variables beyond prespecified moderators into MRT estimation to reduce chance imbalances and improve asymptotic efficiency. Extends to post-treatment auxiliary variables for lagged treatment effects. Demonstrated on Intern Health Study data.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Intern Health Study analysis
papers.notes: Addresses practical issue of underutilized sensor data in MRT analyses.

papers.label: Estimating Causal Effects for Binary Outcomes Using Per-Decision Inverse Probability Weighting
papers.authors: Yihan Bao, Lauren Bell, Elizabeth Williamson, Claire Garnett, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Reduced IPW variance in MRT binary outcomes by 2x+ using per-decision IPW
papers.detail: Proposes per-decision IPW estimators that avoid variance inflation from product weighting when later treatments occur within the outcome window. Second estimator adds semiparametric efficiency projection. Demonstrated substantial efficiency improvement over standard IPW.
papers.notes: Key practical advance for binary proximal outcomes common in mHealth (e.g., app opens).

papers.label: Incorporating nonparametric methods for estimating causal excursion effects in mobile health with zero-inflated count outcomes
papers.authors: Xueqing Liu, Tianchen Qian, Lauren Bell, Bibhas Chakraborty
papers.headline: Extended causal excursion effects to zero-inflated count outcomes with nonparametric estimation
papers.detail: Develops estimation approaches for causal excursion effects with zero-inflated count proximal outcomes. Incorporates nonparametric techniques with bidirectional asymptotics. Motivated by and applied to Drink Less MRT for alcohol reduction.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Drink Less MRT analysis
papers.notes: Addresses a common but previously unaddressed outcome type in MRTs: zero-inflated counts.

papers.label: Efficient and Globally Robust Causal Excursion Effect Estimation
papers.authors: Zhaoxi Cheng, Lauren Bell, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Derived semiparametric efficiency bound for CEE and proposed efficient globally robust estimators
papers.detail: Derives semiparametric efficiency bound for causal excursion effects with identity/log links. Proposes two-stage estimators achieving the efficiency bound and robust to misspecified nuisance models. Establishes general theory for globally robust Z-estimators. Demonstrated substantial efficiency gains on Drink Less MRT data.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Drink Less MRT analysis
papers.notes: Theoretical ceiling: derives the fundamental efficiency bound for CEE estimation.

papers.label: Data integration methods for micro-randomized trials
papers.authors: Easton Huch, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Lindsey Potter, Cho Lam, David W. Wetter, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Developed meta-analytic data integration combining multiple MRTs for efficiency gains
papers.detail: Develops methods to integrate data from multiple related MRTs to improve statistical efficiency. Uses multivariate precision-weighted meta-estimator with asymptotic optimality. Applied to two smoking cessation MRTs.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with two smoking cessation MRTs
papers.effect_size: N/A - methodological; efficiency gains from data integration
papers.notes: Published in Biometrics 2025. First paper to formally address MRT data integration.

papers.label: Modeling Time-Varying Effects of Mobile Health Interventions Using Longitudinal Functional Data from HeartSteps Micro-Randomized Trial
papers.authors: Jiaxin Yu, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: First semiparametric varying-coefficient model for time-varying MRT effects using functional outcomes
papers.detail: Proposes first semiparametric causal excursion effect model with varying coefficients for time-varying effects within and across decision points. Uses double time indices for longitudinal functional step count outcomes. HeartSteps analysis shows prompt effects vary by context (sedentary, weekday, suggestion type).
papers.effect_size: Varies by context; prompts more effective when recently sedentary
papers.notes: Directly relevant to project context: HeartSteps analysis with granular step count outcomes.

papers.label: Doubly Robust Estimation of Causal Excursion Effects in Micro-Randomized Trials with Missing Longitudinal Outcomes
papers.authors: Jiaxin Yu, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: First doubly robust CEE estimator handling MAR missing data in MRTs
papers.detail: Two-stage doubly robust estimator for causal excursion effects when longitudinal outcomes are missing at random. Handles continuous, binary, and count outcomes. Consistent and asymptotically normal if either missingness or outcome model is correct. Demonstrated on HeartSteps data.
papers.notes: Addresses missing data problem pervasive in mHealth MRTs (sensor non-wear, missed self-reports).

papers.label: Evaluation of the HeartSteps Online Sampling Algorithm
papers.authors: Xiang Meng, Walter Dempsey, Peng Liao, Nick Reid, Pedja Klasnja, Susan Murphy
papers.headline: HeartSteps V2V3 online randomization algorithm successfully met intervention frequency and uniformity constraints
papers.detail: Case study evaluating the online sampling algorithm used in HeartSteps V2V3 MRT to manage two constraints: average 1.5 interventions/day and uniform delivery across decision times. Algorithm performed well on both metrics. Provides practical recommendations for constrained MRT randomization.
papers.notes: Directly relevant to HeartSteps constraint handling; important for power-constrained exploration in MRTs.

papers.label: Distal Causal Excursion Effects: Modeling Long-Term Effects of Time-Varying Treatments in Micro-Randomized Trials
papers.authors: Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Introduced distal causal excursion effects (DCEE) for long-term behavior change in MRTs
papers.detail: Novel estimand DCEE quantifies long-term effects of time-varying treatments on distal outcomes. Contrasts outcomes under two excursion policies while marginalizing over most treatment assignments. HeartSteps analysis: prompts delivered earlier in the study had stronger long-term habit formation effects.
papers.effect_size: Earlier prompts had stronger long-term effects on habit formation
papers.notes: Critical for project: bridges MRT proximal effects to long-term behavior change outcomes.

papers.label: Micro-randomized Trials with Categorical Treatments: Causal Effect Estimation and Sample Size Calculation
papers.authors: Jeremy Lin, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Extended WCLS and sample size formulas to multi-categorical MRT treatments
papers.detail: Extends causal excursion effect definition and WCLS estimator to MRTs with categorical (3+) treatment levels. Develops sample size formula for comparing categorical treatment levels with type I error and power guarantees. Provides practical guidelines. Illustrated on HeartSteps.
papers.notes: Directly relevant: 4-action MDP requires multi-categorical treatment methods.

papers.label: Semiparametric Off-Policy Inference for Optimal Policy Values under Possible Non-Uniqueness
papers.authors: Haoyu Wei
papers.headline: Developed NSAVE method for off-policy inference with Drink Less MRT application
papers.detail: Studies inference for optimal policy values in MDPs. Proposes Nonparametric Sequential Value Evaluation (NSAVE) method yielding martingale-based inference with double-robustness. Applies to Drink Less MRT for confidence intervals on state-adaptive notification policies.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Drink Less MRT analysis
papers.notes: Relevant for off-policy evaluation when transitioning from MRT to RL-based policies.

papers.label: Dynamic Causal Mediation Analysis for Intensive Longitudinal Data
papers.authors: Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Introduced natural direct/indirect excursion effects for mediation in MRTs
papers.detail: Proposes natural direct and indirect excursion effects to decompose total excursion effects in MRTs. Derives efficient influence functions and multiply-robust estimators. Applied to HeartSteps MRT and SleepHealth observational study.
papers.notes: Bridges MRT causal inference with mediation analysis for understanding why interventions work.

papers.label: Robust Estimation of Moderated Causal Excursion Odds Ratio in Micro-Randomized Trials
papers.authors: Jiaxin Yu, Tianchen Qian
papers.headline: Doubly robust estimators for moderated causal excursion odds ratio in MRTs
papers.detail: Two estimators for moderated causal excursion odds ratio for binary outcomes. First is doubly robust when moderator captures sequential randomization; second handles general settings with robustness to association model misspecification. Applied to Drink Less MRT.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Drink Less MRT analysis
papers.notes: Provides odds-ratio scale alternative to relative risk for binary proximal outcomes.

papers.label: Doubly Robust Estimation with Stabilized Weights for Binary Proximal Outcomes in Micro-Randomized Trials
papers.authors: Jinho Cha, Eunchan Cha
papers.headline: DR-EMEE achieves 2x efficiency gain over IPW and 5-10% over EMEE for binary MRT outcomes
papers.detail: Proposes doubly robust EMEE with stabilized and truncated weights for binary proximal outcomes. Proves double robustness, asymptotic efficiency. Up to twofold efficiency gains over IPW. Validated on HeartSteps, PAMAP2, and mHealth datasets.
papers.effect_size: 2x efficiency gain over IPW, 5-10% over EMEE
papers.notes: Practical efficiency gains for binary outcomes common in mHealth engagement metrics.

papers.label: Power Constrained Nonstationary Bandits with Habituation and Recovery Dynamics
papers.authors: Fengxu Li, Stephanie M. Carpenter, Matthew P. Buman, Yonatan Mintz
papers.headline: Thompson Sampling with probability clipping balances personalization and power in MRTs
papers.detail: Develops ROGUE-TS for nonstationary bandits with habituation/recovery dynamics. Introduces probability clipping to balance personalization and population-level learning. Validated on two MRT datasets (physical activity, bipolar disorder). Maintains statistical power while lowering regret.
papers.effect_size: Lower regret than baselines; maintains power via clipping
papers.notes: Directly relevant to power-constrained exploration. Bridges bandit algorithms and MRT statistical validity.

papers.label: Prediction Intervals for Individual Treatment Effects in a Multiple Decision Point Framework using Conformal Inference
papers.authors: Swaraj Bose, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Conformal prediction intervals for time-varying ITEs in MRTs with non-exchangeability guarantees
papers.detail: Proposes conformal inference method for prediction intervals of time-varying individual treatment effects. Weaker exchangeability assumptions. Coverage guarantee dependent on non-exchangeability degree. Applied to Intern Health Study MRT.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with Intern Health Study analysis
papers.notes: Extends MRT inference from average to individual-level effects with uncertainty quantification.

papers.label: Evaluating time-varying treatment effects in hybrid SMART-MRT designs
papers.authors: Mengbing Li, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: First causal estimands and analysis methods for hybrid SMART+MRT designs
papers.detail: Formally defines synergistic effects in hybrid SMART-MRT designs integrating human-delivered and digital components at different timescales. Assesses proximal and distal outcomes. Applied to M-Bridge targeting binge drinking reduction in college students.
papers.notes: Relevant for multi-timescale adaptation combining coach and digital components.

papers.label: Estimation of Time-Varying Treatment Effects in a Joint Model for Longitudinal and Recurrent Event Outcomes
papers.authors: Madeline R Abbott, Jeremy M G Taylor, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Lindsey N Potter, David W Wetter, Cho Y Lam, Walter Dempsey
papers.headline: Bayesian joint model handles MRT treatment effects on longitudinal and recurrent event outcomes
papers.detail: Model-based approach for MRT treatment effect estimation via joint longitudinal-survival model. Bayesian inference with information criteria. Demonstrated on MRT of substance use with multiple outcomes and recurrent events.
papers.sample_size: N/A - methodological with substance use MRT data
papers.notes: Joint modeling approach for complex MRT outcomes beyond simple proximal responses.

papers.label: IntelligentPooling: Practical Thompson Sampling for mHealth
papers.authors: Sabina Tomkins, Peng Liao, Predrag Klasnja, Susan Murphy
papers.headline: Practical Thompson sampling contextual bandit for mHealth, deployed in a clinical trial.
papers.detail: Proposes IntelligentPooling for small sample sizes, non-stationarity, and delayed rewards in mHealth.
papers.notes: Core methodology from Murphy lab. Foundation for mHealth bandit deployments.

papers.label: A Contextual-Bandit-Based Approach for Informed Decision-Making in Clinical Trials
papers.authors: Yogatheesan Varatharajah, Brent Berry
papers.headline: CB with Thompson sampling outperforms random assignment in stroke clinical trial simulation.
papers.detail: Evaluated on International Stroke Trial data. 72.63% gain vs random, 64.34% gain vs context-free MAB.
papers.notes: Demonstrates clinical trial optimization using patient contexts.

papers.label: Brief Digital Interventions for Psychological Distress: An AI-Enhanced Response-Adaptive RCT
papers.authors: Jill Newby, Sunil Gupta, Leonard Hoon, et al.
papers.headline: Contextual MAB allocated 1,282 students to digital mental health interventions; physical activity best for severe distress.
papers.detail: AI-enhanced response-adaptive RCT. Physical activity and mindfulness effective for severe distress; physical activity and sleep hygiene for mild distress. Improved trial efficiency.
papers.notes: Landmark bandit clinical trial in mental health. Top medical journal.

papers.label: Protocol for the Vibe Up bandit-based adaptive trial for psychological distress
papers.authors: Kit Huckvale, Leonard Hoon, Eileen Stech, et al.
papers.headline: Protocol for contextual bandit AI-adaptive trial comparing digital interventions for student distress.
papers.detail: Multiarm contextual bandit with up to 12 sequential mini-trials comparing mindfulness, physical activity, sleep hygiene, and control.
papers.notes: Protocol for Vibe Up. Companion to the JAMA Network Open results.

papers.label: CAREForMe: Contextual MAB Recommendation Framework for Mental Health
papers.authors: Sheng Yu, Narjes Nourzad, Randye J. Semple, et al.
papers.headline: Contextual MAB framework for mental health with mobile sensing and online learning.
papers.detail: Integrates mobile sensing, online learning, and user clustering. Implemented across Discord and Telegram.
papers.notes: Framework paper; no clinical deployment.

papers.label: Using Adaptive Bandit Experiments to Increase Engagement in Mental Health
papers.authors: Harsh Kumar, Tong Li, Jiakai Shi, et al.
papers.headline: Thompson Sampling bandit deployed in text-message mental health intervention with 1,100 users.
papers.detail: Two-year system development. Recruited via large mental health non-profit. Compares bandit with uniform random.
papers.notes: Large-scale mental health deployment. Mohr and Williams labs.

papers.label: Offline Contextual MAB for mHealth: A Case Study on Emotion Regulation
papers.authors: Mawulolo K. Ameko, Miranda L. Beltzer, Lihua Cai, et al.
papers.headline: First offline CB recommender for emotion regulation using real mobile health data (n=114).
papers.detail: Doubly robust offline learning algorithms significantly outperformed baselines for emotion regulation strategy recommendation.
papers.notes: Early CB work for mental health. Transdiagnostic emotion regulation focus.

papers.label: Exploring RL for Fluid Transitions Between Clinical Mental Healthcare and Everyday Wellness
papers.authors: Tony Wang, Qian Yang
papers.headline: Contextual bandit for clinical-wellness mental health transitions (N=38); benefits appeared only post-intervention.
papers.detail: Contextual bandit selecting journaling prompts from clinical and wellness repertoires. Benefits of RL optimization emerged only after interventions ended. Raises questions about burnout and treatment intensity.
papers.notes: Important negative finding: bandit-optimized sequences showed delayed benefits.

papers.label: Personalized Glucose Management With AI: Pilot Study Using a Multiarmed Bandit Approach
papers.authors: Shinji Hotta, Mikko Kytö, Saila Koivusalo, Seppo Heinonen, Pekka Marttinen
papers.headline: Multi-armed bandit for postprandial glucose management shows 23% improvement in real-world pilot (n=6).
papers.detail: Two-stage reward prediction model predicts dietary and exercise effects on glucose. Simulation study plus 6-person pilot.
papers.notes: Early-stage diabetes bandit. Small but promising pilot.

papers.label: Equitable Intervention Planning with RMAB for Type 2 Diabetes Digital Health
papers.authors: Jackson A. Killian, Manish Jain, Yugang Jia, et al.
papers.headline: Restless MAB with equity constraints for T2D: 10% more patients reach targets, 85% less disparity.
papers.detail: RMAB with equitable bilevel optimization. 10% improvement in glucose targets, 10% reduction in dropout, 85% reduction in disparities across 6 demographic groups.
papers.notes: Important equity-focused bandit. Google-Verily-Harvard collaboration.

papers.label: Practical considerations for online learning algorithm in app-based mHealth (LOWSALT4LIFE 2)
papers.authors: Rachel T. Gonzalez, Madeline R. Abbott, Brahmajee Nallamothu, et al.
papers.headline: LOWSALT4LIFE 2 trial: contextual bandit for hypertension app engagement; deployment challenges documented.
papers.detail: RL algorithm sends notifications to reduce sodium intake. Documents challenges: reward definition, timescale, model specification, missing data, computational cost.
papers.notes: Important practical deployment paper. Hypertension domain. University of Michigan.

papers.label: REINFORCE trial: RL to personalize communication on medication adherence
papers.authors: John C. Lauffenburger, Elad Yom-Tov, Punam A. Keller, et al.
papers.headline: REINFORCE trial: RL-personalized communication significantly improved medication adherence at 6 months.
papers.detail: Multi-arm bandit RL trial testing personalized messages for medication adherence. Found significant improvements over 6-month follow-up.
papers.notes: Landmark bandit trial for medication adherence. Top digital health journal.

papers.label: RL for persuading to quit smoking and be physically active
papers.authors: N. Albers, M.A. Neerincx, W.P. Brinkman
papers.headline: RL algorithm modeling current and future states for smoking cessation and physical activity persuasion.
papers.detail: Models both current and future user states to personalize smoking cessation and physical activity persuasion strategies.
papers.notes: Smoking cessation bandit/RL. Combines smoking and physical activity.

papers.label: A Bayesian-bandit adaptive design for N-of-1 clinical trials
papers.authors: S. Shrestha, S. Jain
papers.headline: Thompson sampling bandit adaptive design for N-of-1 clinical trials.
papers.detail: Bayesian bandit using TS for individualized treatment comparisons within single patients.
papers.notes: N-of-1 trial design. Relevant for personalized medicine.

papers.label: Multi-disciplinary fairness considerations in ML for clinical trials
papers.authors: Irene Chien, Nina Deliu, Rebecca Turner, Adrian Weller, Sofia Villar, et al.
papers.headline: Multidisciplinary analysis of fairness in ML for clinical trials, including bandit algorithms.
papers.detail: Addresses fairness in bandit algorithm design, multiple testing, and ethical guidelines for clinical trials.
papers.notes: Directly addresses ethical concerns with bandits in clinical trials.

papers.label: Truthful mechanisms for linear bandit games with private contexts
papers.authors: Yiting Hu, Lingjie Duan
papers.headline: Shows UCB and TS fail under context misreporting in clinical trials; proposes truthful mechanism.
papers.detail: First study of private context misreporting in CB. Shows standard algorithms fail in clinical trial settings. Proposes LP-based mechanism with O(ln T) regret.
papers.notes: Important negative result: failure modes of standard bandits under strategic misreporting.

papers.label: A New Bandit Setting Balancing State Evolution and Corrupted Context
papers.authors: Alexander Galozy, Slawomir Nowaczyk, Mattias Ohlsson
papers.headline: Novel bandit combining contextual and state-transition info, evaluated on simulated medication adherence.
papers.detail: Combines contextual bandit and MAB via referee. Motivated by mHealth where user internal states are partially observable.
papers.notes: Handles partially observable internal states and unreliable context in mHealth.

papers.label: Optimal dose selection in phase I/II trials with contextual bandits
papers.authors: Jixian Wang, Ram Tiwari
papers.headline: CB with TS for oncology dose-finding; practical recommendations for industry adoption.
papers.detail: Extensive simulation comparing TS, Bayesian bootstrap, and MAB approaches for dose-finding. Practical recommendations provided.
papers.notes: BMS paper showing industry CB adoption for dose-finding.

papers.label: Learn to Intervene: Adaptive Learning for Restless Bandits in Preventive Healthcare
papers.authors: Arpita Biswas, Gaurav Aggarwal, Pradeep Varakantham, Milind Tambe
papers.headline: Restless bandit adaptive policy for medication adherence in diabetes and hypertension.
papers.detail: Adaptive learning policy for health intervention allocation in resource-constrained settings. Evaluated on Chiapas Mexico data.
papers.notes: Tambe lab. Restless bandits for global health.

papers.label: Optimizing HIV Patient Engagement with RL in Resource-Limited Settings
papers.authors: Álvaro Periáñez, K. Schmitz, L. Makhupula, et al.
papers.headline: Bandit algorithms for HIV patient engagement and treatment adherence in underserved populations.
papers.detail: Applies bandit algorithms to optimize HIV patient engagement in resource-limited Sub-Saharan African contexts.
papers.notes: Global health bandit application for HIV.

papers.label: Spoiled for choice? Personalized recommendation for healthcare: A multiarmed bandit approach
papers.authors: Tao Zhou, Yuqing Wang, Lu Yan, Yong Tan
papers.headline: Contextual bandit with diversity constraints for healthcare decision recommendations.
papers.detail: Multiarmed bandit for personalized healthcare recommendations with diversity constraints. Information systems perspective.
papers.notes: IS perspective on CB for healthcare recommendations.

papers.label: Risk-inclusive Contextual Bandits for Early Phase Clinical Trials
papers.authors: R. Kanrar, C. Li, Z. Ghodsi, M. Gamalo
papers.headline: Risk-inclusive CB with Thompson sampling for early phase clinical trials (Pfizer).
papers.detail: Multi-objective optimization with risk constraints. Leverages TS and online RL for early phase trials.
papers.notes: Industry (Pfizer) risk-aware bandits for clinical trials.

papers.label: Optimizing Digital Therapeutic Interventions under Endogenous Adherence
papers.authors: E. Pulick, S. Carpenter, M. Buman, Y. Mintz
papers.headline: UCB-BOLD: contextual bandit for digital therapeutics accounting for endogenous adherence.
papers.detail: Addresses the challenge that adherence is affected by the treatment itself (endogeneity).
papers.notes: Addresses endogeneity challenge in digital therapeutic bandits.

papers.label: Planning to fairly allocate: Probabilistic fairness in restless bandits
papers.authors: Christina Herlihy, A. Prins, A. Srinivasan, et al.
papers.headline: Probabilistic fairness framework for restless bandits in health intervention allocation.
papers.detail: Addresses ethical concerns about disparities from bandit-based health resource allocation. Provides fairness guarantees.
papers.notes: Fairness/ethics of bandit allocation in healthcare.

papers.label: Accelerated TS for Response-Adaptive Trials
papers.authors: Wang J
papers.headline: NEGATIVE: TS disappointing in clinical trials; acceleration proposed
papers.detail: Reports multiple simulations show TS fails in clinical trial settings. Proposes accelerated TS for short-run improvement.

papers.label: Top-Two TS for Best Treatment ID
papers.authors: Wang J, Tiwari R
papers.headline: TTTS with acceleration for small-sample drug development
papers.detail: Extends TTTS to drug development small-sample regime. Introduces simpler TTTS2 variant.

papers.label: TS for SMART Designs
papers.authors: Norwood P, Davidian M, Laber E
papers.headline: First TS-based RAR for multi-stage SMARTs
papers.detail: First RAR algorithms for SMARTs using TS. Valid post-study inference for non-standard limiting behavior.

papers.label: Finite-Sample Error for RAR
papers.authors: Deliu N, Villar SS
papers.headline: When TS-based inference fails: finite-sample analysis
papers.detail: Error control of randomization tests under TS-based RAR. Conditions where standard inference fails identified.

papers.label: MABs for Dose-Finding
papers.authors: Kojima M
papers.headline: TS vs UCB vs Gittins for dose-finding trials
papers.detail: Systematic MAB evaluation including TS for dose-finding. TS balances patient benefit and correct ID.

papers.label: Replicable Bandits for Digital Health
papers.authors: Zhang KW, Closser A, Trella AL, Murphy SA
papers.headline: TS variants ensuring valid post-study inference
papers.detail: Replicable bandits including TS variants guaranteeing error control for treatment effect estimation.

papers.label: Causal Inference and Bandits for Digital Health
papers.authors: Svihrova R, Dei Rossi A, Marzorati D, et al.
papers.headline: Review: TS open problems in digital health
papers.detail: Reviews TS variants, exploration, causal inference. Open problems: non-stationarity, heterogeneity.

papers.label: TS for TB Screening in Peru
papers.authors: Zhang J, Linde L, Puma D, et al.
papers.headline: Deployed TS for mobile TB screening in Lima
papers.detail: TS-based bandit optimizes mobile TB screening locations. Increases case detection efficiency.

papers.label: Bayesian RAR for Cluster RCTs
papers.authors: Liu Y, Young Karris M, Jain S
papers.headline: TS-based RAR extended to cluster-randomized trials
papers.detail: Extends Bayesian RAR with TS to CRCTs addressing intra-cluster correlation.

papers.label: TS for Non-Stationary Bandits
papers.authors: Qi H, Guo F, Zhu L
papers.headline: TS with change-point detection for non-stationarity
papers.detail: TS with change-point detection and sliding-window posteriors. Provides regret bounds.

papers.label: Bandits for COVID-19 Case-Finding
papers.authors: Rayo MF, Faulkner D, Kline D, et al.
papers.headline: TS deployed for COVID-19 testing in Ohio
papers.detail: TS bandits for adaptive testing site allocation. Improves over heuristic methods.

papers.label: Bandits with Volume Constraints
papers.authors: Warren JL, Prunas O, Paltiel AD, et al.
papers.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series A
papers.headline: TS with resource constraints for surveillance
papers.detail: Integrates testing volume constraints into TS bandits for disease surveillance.

papers.label: Bayesian Optimization for TMS
papers.authors: Schultheiss DL, Turi Z, Boedecker J, Vlachos A
papers.headline: Bayesian optimization reduces TMS patient burden
papers.detail: GP-based Bayesian optimization for TMS coil positioning; fewer stimulations than grid search.

papers.label: Bayesian m-Top for Vaccine Policy
papers.authors: Cimpean A, Verstraeten T, Willem L, et al.
papers.headline: Bayesian exploration for COVID-19 vaccine policy
papers.detail: Bayesian m-top exploration (TS-related) for vaccine allocation policy evaluation.

papers.label: Optimal Allocation in Adaptive Designs
papers.authors: Yi Y, Wang X
papers.headline: Theoretical: TS vs optimal allocation targets
papers.detail: Compares TS-based allocation to optimal Neyman allocation.

papers.label: Bayesian Strategy for GP Prescribing
papers.authors: Allender S, Hayward J, Gupta S, et al.
papers.headline: Bayesian optimization for healthcare strategy
papers.detail: GP-based Bayesian optimization identifies optimal healthcare strategies.

papers.label: MAB Backfill BOIN
papers.authors: Kojima M, Takeda K
papers.headline: TS-MAB with Bayesian optimal interval design
papers.detail: Integrates TS-based MAB with BOIN for dose-finding with backfill.

papers.label: Mixed-Effects Bandit for mHealth
papers.authors: Huch EK, Shi J, Abbott MR, et al.
papers.headline: Mixed-effects TS for user heterogeneity in mHealth
papers.detail: Robust mixed-effects TS accounting for between-user heterogeneity in mobile health.

papers.label: Bayesian RAR with Binary Outcomes
papers.authors: Proper J, Connett J, Murray T
papers.headline: Comparison of Bayesian RAR including TS-like allocation
papers.detail: Evaluates Bayesian RAR techniques for binary outcomes, comparing TS-like posterior probability allocation against alternatives.

papers.label: Model Selection for Offline Reinforcement Learning: Practical Considerations for Healthcare Settings
papers.authors: Shengpu Tang, Jenna Wiens
papers.detail: Investigates model selection for offline RL in healthcare using OPE as a proxy for validation. Finds FQE consistently leads to best validation ranking. Proposes a two-stage approach to accelerate model selection.

papers.label: Offline Reinforcement Learning with Uncertainty for Treatment Strategies in Sepsis
papers.authors: Ran Liu, Joseph L. Greenstein, James C. Fackler, Jules Bergmann, Melania M. Bembea, Raimond L. Winslow
papers.detail: Novel application of RL for sepsis treatment recommendations with confidence estimation. Identifies RL bias against aggressive intervention due to confounding. Mitigates bias using subspace learning.

papers.label: Proximal Reinforcement Learning: Efficient Off-Policy Evaluation in Partially Observed Markov Decision Processes
papers.authors: Andrew Bennett, Nathan Kallus
papers.detail: Extends proximal causal inference to POMDPs for off-policy evaluation. Introduces bridge functions for identification. Demonstrates on sepsis management.

papers.label: Multi-Objective SPIBB: Seldonian Offline Policy Improvement with Safety Constraints in Finite MDPs
papers.authors: Harsh Satija, Philip S. Thomas, Joelle Pineau, Romain Laroche
papers.detail: Safe Policy Improvement under constraints with multiple rewards. Provides high-probability guarantees. Evaluated on IV fluids and vasopressors for sepsis treatment.

papers.label: Semi-Markov Offline Reinforcement Learning for Healthcare
papers.authors: Mehdi Fatemi, Mary Wu, Jeremy Petch, Walter Nelson, Stuart J. Connolly, Alexander Benz, Anthony Carnicelli, Marzyeh Ghassemi
papers.detail: Proposes Semi-MDP framework for offline RL with variable-time actions. Introduces SDQN, SDDQN, SBCQ algorithms. Demonstrates MDP methods fail on variable-time environments. Validated on warfarin dosing.

papers.label: Towards Safe Mechanical Ventilation Treatment Using Deep Offline Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Flemming Kondrup, Thomas Jiralerspong, Elaine Lau, Nathan de Lara, Jacob Shkrob, My Duc Tran, Doina Precup, Sumana Basu
papers.detail: DeepVent: CQL-based agent for ventilator parameter optimization. Clinically relevant intermediate reward addresses sparse rewards. Recommends parameters within safe ranges per clinical trials.

papers.label: Offline Reinforcement Learning for Safer Blood Glucose Control in People with Type 1 Diabetes
papers.authors: Harry Emerson, Matthew Guy, Ryan McConville
papers.detail: Evaluates BCQ, CQL and TD3-BC for insulin dosing. Trained on less than a tenth of online RL samples. Increases time in healthy range from 61.6% to 65.3% without increasing hypoglycemia.

papers.label: Deep Offline Reinforcement Learning for Real-world Treatment Optimization Applications
papers.authors: Milashini Nambiar, Supriyo Ghosh, Priscilla Ong, Yu En Chan, Yong Mong Bee, Pavitra Krishnaswamy
papers.detail: Introduces transition sampling to address action imbalance in offline RL. Compares DDQN vs CQL on diabetes and sepsis tasks. Shows substantial improvements over standard CQL.

papers.label: Delphic Offline Reinforcement Learning under Nonidentifiable Hidden Confounding
papers.authors: Alizée Pace, Hugo Yèche, Bernhard Schölkopf, Gunnar Rätsch, Guy Tennenholtz
papers.detail: Tackles hidden confounding in nonidentifiable offline RL settings. Proposes delphic uncertainty from world model variation. Constructs pessimistic offline RL algorithm. Validated on sepsis benchmark and EHR.

papers.label: Towards Real-World Applications of Personalized Anesthesia Using Policy Constraint Q Learning for Propofol Infusion Control
papers.authors: Xiuding Cai, Jiao Chen, Yaoyao Zhu, Beimin Wang, Yu Yao
papers.detail: Proposes Policy Constraint Q-Learning (PCQL) for anesthesia. Adds policy constraint to CQL to keep agent distribution close to anesthesiologist. SHAP interpretability analysis.

papers.label: Reinforcement Learning in Dynamic Treatment Regimes Needs Critical Reexamination
papers.authors: Zhiyao Luo, Yangchen Pan, Peter Watkinson, Tingting Zhu
papers.detail: Critical examination of offline RL in DTRs. With 17,000+ experiments on sepsis data, shows RL performance varies significantly with evaluation metrics and MDP formulations. RL can be surpassed by random baselines.

papers.label: Empowering Clinicians with Medical Decision Transformers: A Framework for Sepsis Treatment
papers.authors: Aamer Abdul Rahman, Pranav Agarwal, Rita Noumeir, Philippe Jouvet, Vincent Michalski, Samira Ebrahimi Kahou
papers.detail: Medical Decision Transformer (MeDT) based on goal-conditioned RL. Uses acuity scores for conditioning. Enables clinician-model interaction. Competitive with CQL, BCQ while more interpretable.

papers.label: Offline Guarded Safe Reinforcement Learning for Medical Treatment Optimization Strategies
papers.authors: Runze Yan, Xun Shen, Akifumi Wachi, Sebastien Gros, Anni Zhao, Xiao Hu
papers.detail: Proposes OGSRL with dual constraint mechanism: OOD guardian for clinically validated regions and safety cost constraint encoding physiological boundaries. Theoretical guarantees on safety and near-optimality.

papers.label: Hybrid Adaptive Conformal Offline Reinforcement Learning for Fair Population Health Management
papers.authors: Sanjay Basu, Sadiq Y. Patel, Parth Sheth, Bhairavi Muralidharan, Namrata Elamaran, Aakriti Kinra, Rajaie Batniji
papers.detail: HACO framework: separates risk calibration from preference optimization. Uses conformal threshold for safe action masking. Validated on 2.77M decisions across 168K patients. AUC ~0.81 for risk discrimination.

papers.label: From Robotics to Sepsis Treatment: Offline RL via Geometric Pessimism
papers.authors: Sarthak Wanjari
papers.detail: Geo-IQL: augments IQL with kNN-based density penalty for OOD conservatism. On MIMIC-III sepsis, standard IQL collapses to BC (75% agreement) while Geo-IQL achieves 86.4% terminal agreement.

papers.label: Ensuring Safety in Automated Mechanical Ventilation through Offline Reinforcement Learning and Digital Twin Verification
papers.authors: Hang Yu, Huidong Liu, Qingchen Zhang, William Joy, Kateryna Nikulina, Andreas A. Schuppert, Sina Saffaran, Declan Bates
papers.detail: Transformer-based CQL (T-CQL) with uncertainty quantification for mechanical ventilation. Digital twin for online bedside evaluation. Outperforms state-of-the-art offline RL methods.

papers.label: Insulin4RL: Real-Time Insulin Management in the Intensive Care Unit for Offline Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Thomas Frost, Steve Harris
papers.detail: Insulin4RL dataset: naturally irregular inputs and actions from MIMIC-IV. 375K+ decisions across 12,209 patients for insulin infusion titration. Baseline metrics using model-free offline RL.

papers.label: Synthetic Acute Hypotension and Sepsis Datasets Based on MIMIC-III and Published as Part of the Health Gym Project
papers.authors: Nicholas I-Hsien Kuo, Mark Polizzotto, Simon Finfer, Louisa Jorm, Sebastiano Barbieri
papers.detail: Synthetic datasets for acute hypotension and sepsis using GANs and MIMIC-III. Very low identity disclosure risk (0.045%). Published as part of the Health Gym project for offline RL development.

papers.label: Offline Policy Evaluation and Optimization under Confounding
papers.authors: Chinmaya Kausik, Yangyi Lu, Kevin Tan, Maggie Makar, Yixin Wang, Ambuj Tewari
papers.detail: Maps landscape of offline policy evaluation under confounding. Characterizes settings where consistent estimates are provably impossible. Provides lower bound estimation algorithms. Evaluated on sepsis management.

papers.label: Predicting the Need for Blood Transfusion in Intensive Care Units with Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Yuqing Wang, Yun Zhao, Linda Petzold
papers.detail: Uses discretized BCQ for transfusion decision-making. Evaluates state representation and reward design. Transfer learning improves performance on data-scarce datasets by up to 17%.

papers.label: Horizon Reduction as Information Loss in Offline Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Uday Kumar Nidadala, Venkata Bhumika Guthi
papers.detail: Proves horizon reduction in offline RL can induce fundamental information loss. Identifies three structural failure modes: prefix indistinguishability, objective misspecification, and representation aliasing. Optimal policies may be indistinguishable from suboptimal ones even with infinite data.

papers.label: Optimizing Warfarin Dosing Using Contextual Bandit: An Offline Policy Learning and Evaluation Method
papers.authors: Yong Huang, Charles A. Downs, Amir M. Rahmani
papers.detail: Uses offline policy learning in contextual bandit setting for warfarin dosing. Learned policies surpass baselines without genotype inputs, even with suboptimal demonstrations.

papers.label: Flexible Blood Glucose Control: Offline Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
papers.authors: Harry Emerson, Sam Gordon James, Matthew Guy, Ryan McConville
papers.detail: PAINT framework: offline RL from human feedback for insulin dosing. Uses sketch-based reward learning from patient records. Reduces glycaemic risk by 15% over commercial benchmark.

papers.label: Guardian-regularized Safe Offline Reinforcement Learning for Smart Weaning of Mechanical Circulatory Devices
papers.authors: Aysin Tumay, Sophia Sun, Sonia Fereidooni, Aaron Dumas, Elise Jortberg, Rose Yu
papers.detail: Clinically-aware OOD-regularized Model-based Policy Optimization (CORMPO) for weaning MCS devices. Transformer-based digital twin for evaluation. 28% higher reward than baselines and 82.6% higher in clinical metrics.

papers.label: Toward Foundation Model for Multivariate Wearable Sensing of Physiological Signals (NormWear)
papers.authors: Yunfei Luo, Yuliang Chen, Asif Salekin, Tauhidur Rahman
papers.headline: First multimodal wearable foundation model (NormWear) using channel-aware attention with CLS token, pretrained on PPG/ECG/EEG/GSR/IMU, evaluated across 11 datasets and 18 applications.
papers.relevance: Core reference for NormWear 768-dim embeddings from PPG/accelerometer. Directly relevant to the next-step Sensor Foundation Model goal.

papers.label: Toward World Modeling of Physiological Signals with Chaos-Theoretic Balancing and Latent Dynamics (NormWear-2)
papers.authors: Yunfei Luo, Xi Chen, Yuliang Chen, Lanshuang Zhang, Md Mofijul Islam, Siwei Zhao, Peter Kotanko, Subhasis Dasgupta, Andrew Campbell, Rakesh Malhotra, Tauhidur Rahman
papers.headline: NormWear-2 extends the foundation model approach to world modeling; forecasts physiological signals across multiple time scales using chaos-theoretic balancing and latent state transition adaptation.
papers.relevance: Direct successor to NormWear; demonstrates forecasting and intervention-conditioned modeling for physiological time series.

papers.label: Scaling Wearable Foundation Models (LSM)
papers.authors: Girish Narayanswamy, Xin Liu, Kumar Ayush, Yuzhe Yang, Xuhai Xu, Shun Liao, Jake Garrison, Shyam Tailor, Jake Sunshine, Yun Liu, Tim Althoff, Shrikanth Narayanan, Pushmeet Kohli, Jiening Zhan, Mark Malhotra, Shwetak Patel, Samy Abdel-Ghaffar, Daniel McDuff
papers.headline: LSM: a multimodal foundation model trained on 40M hours of wearable sensor data from 165K people. Establishes scaling laws for imputation, interpolation, and extrapolation.
papers.relevance: Key reference for wearable foundation model scaling; includes imputation as a core task; largest wearable-signals dataset to date.

papers.label: Physical activities enable scalable foundation modelling for broad-spectrum health prediction (StepFM)
papers.authors: Zhenghuang Wu, Yuyao Zhu, Songlin Xu
papers.headline: StepFM: a foundation model built solely on step counter data. Privacy-preserving, computation-efficient. Transferred to >20 health risk prediction tasks.
papers.relevance: Demonstrates low-dimensional step data as viable foundation model input with privacy benefits.

papers.label: Inertia-1: An Open Exploration of Wearable Motion Foundation Models
papers.authors: Zongzhe Xu, Aakarsh Anand, Sarah Jiang, Chuntung Zhuang, Zitao Shuai, Sriram Sankararaman, Yuzhe Yang
papers.headline: Comprehensive exploration using >18.2M hours of accelerometer data. Evaluates data choices, model choices, and training choices across 15 datasets.
papers.relevance: Provides design recipes for motion foundation models; key resource for building accelerometer-based foundation models.

papers.label: SiamQuality: A ConvNet-Based Foundation Model for Imperfect Physiological Signals
papers.authors: Cheng Ding, Zhicheng Guo, Zhaoliang Chen, Randall J Lee, Cynthia Rudin, Xiao Hu
papers.headline: SIAMQUALITY uses Siamese self-supervised learning on 36M PPG pairs for robust representations. CNN backbone outperforms transformers on imperfect physiological data.
papers.relevance: Directly addresses signal quality in foundation model training; robust to noisy and incomplete physiological data.

papers.label: A robust PPG foundation model using multimodal physiological supervision
papers.authors: Eloy Geenjaar, Vince Calhoun, Scott Daly, Gouthaman KV, Lie Lu, Trisha Mittal, Daniel P. Darcy
papers.headline: PPG foundation model leveraging ECG/respiratory signals for contrastive pretraining. Handles noisy PPG without requiring high-quality pretraining data.
papers.relevance: Multimodal supervision for robust PPG representation; improved 14/15 downstream tasks with 3x fewer subjects.

papers.label: Multiple Imputation Approaches for Epoch-level Accelerometer data in Trials
papers.authors: Mia S. Tackney, Elizabeth Williamson, Derek G. Cook, Elizabeth Limb, Tess Harris, James Carpenter
papers.headline: Epoch-level (5-second) missing data framework for accelerometer trials. Parametric and non-parametric MI approaches. Validated on PACE-UP Trial.
papers.relevance: Directly addresses missing accelerometer data in clinical trials with epoch-level granularity.

papers.label: A framework for handling missing accelerometer outcome data in trials
papers.authors: Mia S. Tackney, Derek G. Cook, Daniel Stahl, Khalida Ismail, Elizabeth Williamson, James Carpenter
papers.headline: Analysis framework using wear time to define missingness on the epoch level; wear-time-informed MI for accelerometer outcomes.
papers.relevance: Practical framework for non-wear and missing data handling in accelerometer-based trials.

papers.label: DynImp: Dynamic Imputation for Wearable Sensing Data Through Sensory and Temporal Relatedness
papers.authors: Zepeng Huo, Taowei Ji, Yifei Liang, Shuai Huang, Zhangyang Wang, Xiaoning Qian, Bobak Mortazavi
papers.headline: DynImp uses nearest-neighbor feature-axis imputation plus LSTM-based denoising autoencoder. Tested at >50% missing rates.
papers.relevance: Handles extreme missingness by leveraging cross-sensor correlations and temporal dynamics.

papers.label: VCR: Learning Valid Contextual Representation for Incomplete Wearable Signals
papers.authors: Yuxuan Weng, Wenhan Luo, Qijia Shao
papers.headline: Self-supervised framework using orthogonal tokenizer and missing-aware Mixture-of-Experts. Reconstructs only shared components to avoid hallucination.
papers.relevance: Novel missing modality handling for wearable data; avoids hallucinating non-inferable modality details.

papers.label: Self-Supervised and Topological Signal-Quality Assessment for Any PPG Device
papers.authors: Wei Shao, Ruoyu Zhang, Zequan Liang, Ehsan Kourkchi, Setareh Rafatirad, Houman Homayoun
papers.headline: First fully unsupervised PPG SQA combining contrastive 1D-ResNet with topological data analysis. Cross-device without re-tuning.
papers.relevance: Unsupervised quality assessment working across heterogeneous PPG devices; no labeled data required.

papers.label: Lightweight ResNet-Based Deep Learning for Photoplethysmography Signal Quality Assessment
papers.authors: Yangyang Zhao, Matti Kaisti, Olli Lahdenoja, Jonas Sandelin, Arman Anzanpour, Joonas Lehto, Joel Nuotio, Jussi Jaakkola, Arto Relander, Tuija Vasankari, Juhani Airaksinen, Tuomas Kiviniemi, Tero Koivisto
papers.headline: Lightweight ResNet with Squeeze-and-Excitation for PPG SQA. >99% parameter reduction; 96.52% AUC on M4M dataset.
papers.relevance: Efficient PPG quality assessment suitable for on-device wearable deployment.

papers.label: Reduction of motion artifacts from photoplethysmography signals using learned convolutional sparse coding
papers.authors: Giulio Basso, Xi Long, Reinder Haakma, Rik Vullings

papers.label: Validation of Garmin and Polar devices for continuous heart rate monitoring during common training movements in tactical populations
papers.authors: Merrigan JJ, Stovall JH, Stone JD, Stephenson MD, Feairheller DL, Galster SM, Hagen JA
papers.venue: Measurement in Physical Education and Exercise Science
papers.headline: Garmin Fenix 6 showed acceptable HR accuracy during steady-state (MAE 4.1 bpm) but errors increased during HIIT and resistance exercises (MAE > 12 bpm).

papers.label: Continuous monitoring of heart rate variability in free-living conditions using wearable sensors: exploratory observational study
papers.authors: Gaur P, Temple DS, Hegarty-Craver M, Sazonov E, Presti P, Bunea I, Ifteni P
papers.headline: Garmin Fenix 6 provided consistent HRV data in free-living conditions but had 23% data loss during sleep. Garmin API required proprietary Health API with limited historical data retrieval.

papers.label: Limiting racial disparities and bias for wearable devices in health science research
papers.authors: Colvonen PJ, DeYoung PN, Bosompra NO, Owens RL
papers.headline: Wearable devices systematically underperform in individuals with darker skin tones, higher BMI, and older age. PPG-based heart rate and SpO2 measurements show significant bias across demographic groups.

papers.label: Demographic imbalances resulting from the bring-your-own-device study design
papers.authors: Cho PJ, Yi J, Ho E, Shandhi MMH, Dinh Y, Patil S, Ventrella J, Klein L, Singh K, Dunn J
papers.headline: BYOD studies disproportionately enroll iPhone users (78% vs 55% population share) and higher-income participants, creating systematic demographic bias in digital health research.

papers.label: Patterns of ownership and usage of wearable devices in the United States, 2020-2022: survey study
papers.authors: Nagappan A, Krasniansky A, Knowles M
papers.headline: US wearable ownership reached 35% by 2022 with significant disparities: 48% high-income vs 18% low-income, 42% white vs 28% Black and 26% Hispanic.

papers.label: Assessment of ownership of smart devices and the acceptability of digital health data sharing
papers.authors: Shandhi MMH, Singh K, Janson N, Ashar P, Cho PJ, Dinh Y, Patil S, Ventrella J, Klein L, Lee S, Dunn J
papers.headline: Only 40% of US adults own a smart wearable. Willingness to share data: 67% with researchers but only 32% with insurers. Privacy concerns were the top barrier for non-owners.

papers.label: Wearable devices in healthcare: Privacy and information security issues
papers.authors: Cilliers L
papers.headline: Most wearable devices and cloud platforms lack adequate encryption and consent mechanisms. Only 23% of fitness tracker apps had comprehensive privacy policies.

papers.label: Does deidentification of data from wearable devices give us a false sense of security? A systematic review
papers.authors: Chikwetu L, Miao Y, Woldetensae MK, Bell D, Goldenholz DM, Dunn J
papers.headline: Standard deidentification is insufficient for wearable data. Re-identification rates of 60-96% using step count and heart rate time series alone. Regulatory frameworks inadequately address re-identification risk.

papers.label: Ethical considerations for the use of consumer wearables in health research
papers.authors: Sui A, Sui W, Liu S, Rhodes R
papers.headline: Consumer wearable platforms have complex data sharing agreements permitting commercial use of research data. Most studies using wearable APIs do not adequately inform participants about third-party data access.

papers.label: Data interoperability for ambulatory monitoring of cardiovascular disease: a scientific statement from the American Heart Association
papers.authors: Armoundas AA, Ahmad FS, Bennett DA, Breathett K, Chung MK, Dimarco JP, Elkind MS, Gluckman TJ, Goldberger ZD, Kuzniewicz M, Maron DJ, Martinez MW, Tereshchenko LG, Tison GH, Turakhia MP
papers.headline: Lack of interoperability between Apple HealthKit, Google Fit, Fitbit, and Garmin is a major barrier to clinical adoption. FHIR-based standards and Health Connect are promising but incomplete solutions.

papers.label: A scalable, secure, and interoperable platform for deep data-driven health management
papers.authors: Bahmani A, Alavi A, Buergel T, Upadhyayula S, Wang Q, Chatterjee R, Srinivasan S, Ravichandran S, Li Y, Huo S, Rodriguez B, Jain K, Hemberg M, Bhatt DL, Dunn J
papers.headline: The Personal Health Dashboard platform integrated data from Apple HealthKit, Google Fit, Fitbit, Garmin, and EHRs. Custom adapters were required for each platform due to incompatible data schemas and API rate limits.

papers.label: Wearable sensors with possibilities for data exchange: Analyzing status and needs of different actors in mobile health monitoring systems
papers.authors: Muzny M, Henriksen A, Giordanengo A, Muzik J, Hartvigsen G, Torsvik T, Grimsgaard S, Hopstock LA, Strandberg RB
papers.headline: Only 38% of consumer wearable devices offered API access for research. Fitbit and Garmin provided richer data via APIs but with rate limits. Apple HealthKit and Google Fit acted primarily as data aggregators.

papers.label: Challenges and recommendations for wearable devices in digital health: Data quality, interoperability, health equity, fairness
papers.authors: Canali S, Schiaffonati V, Aliverti A
papers.headline: Four major challenges: (1) poor data quality from consumer-grade sensors, (2) lack of cross-platform interoperability, (3) demographic bias in training data causing algorithmic fairness issues, (4) insufficient regulatory oversight.

papers.label: Data quality evaluation in wearable monitoring
papers.authors: Bottcher S, Vieluf S, Bruno E, Joseph B, Epitashvili N, Marschitz T, Reinsberger C
papers.headline: Proposed a standardized data quality evaluation framework. Found 15-30% of wearable data in free-living conditions is corrupted by motion artifacts. PPG-based sensors were most affected.

papers.label: On general data protection regulation vulnerabilities and privacy issues, for wearable devices and fitness tracking applications
papers.authors: Ioannidou I, Sklavos N
papers.headline: Security analysis of 12 fitness trackers: 8 transmitted unencrypted data. Multiple platforms leaked personal identifiers through API endpoints. GDPR compliance was incomplete for most platforms.
